The Star (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $40,847,995Details
International Box Office $21,744,143
Worldwide Box Office $62,592,138
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,530,749
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,103,974
Total $12,634,723
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A small but brave donkey named Bo yearns for a life beyond his daily grind at the village mill. One day he finds the courage to break free, and finally goes on the adventure of his dreams. On his journey, he teams up with Ruth, a lovable sheep who has lost her flock and Dave, a dove with lofty aspirations. Along with three wisecracking camels and some eccentric stable animals, Bo and his new friends follow the Star and become unlikely heroes in the greatest story ever told—the first Christmas.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,812,674 (24.0% of total gross)
Legs:4.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,837 opening theaters/2,976 max. theaters, 5.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $54,445,878

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 17th, 2017 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: November 15th, 2017 (Wide) (France)
November 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Aruba)
November 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Curacao)
November 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Iraq)
November 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Lebanon)
Video Release: February 6th, 2018 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2495 (Cert #50947), 9/27/2017)
Running Time: 86 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Plot point: Religious, Talking Animals
Release detail: Christmas in November
Social setting: Christmas
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Source:Based on Religious Text
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Sony Pictures Animation, Franklin Entertainment, Walden Media, Jim Henson Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A small but brave donkey named Bo yearns for a life beyond his daily grind at the village mill. One day he finds the courage to break free, and finally goes on the adventure of his dreams. On his journey, he teams up with Ruth, a lovable sheep who has lost her flock and Dave, a dove with lofty aspirations. Along with three wisecracking camels and some eccentric stable animals, Bo and his new friends follow the Star and become unlikely heroes in the greatest story ever told—the first Christmas.
